context: Tests whether the agent implements US economic nexus tracking with state-specific thresholds (CA, TX, NY, default), fires an alert when nexus is triggered, sends early warnings at 80% of the threshold, and uses an upsert pattern to update running totals.

checklist.name: CA/TX $500k threshold
checklist.description: California and Texas are assigned a $500,000 sales threshold (not the default $100,000)

checklist.name: CA/TX no txn threshold
checklist.description: California and Texas have no transaction count threshold (null or not checked)

checklist.name: NY $500k + 100 txns
checklist.description: New York uses $500,000 sales threshold AND 100 transaction count threshold

checklist.name: Default $100k/200 txns
checklist.description: All other states default to $100,000 sales OR 200 transactions threshold

checklist.name: Triggered alert subject
checklist.description: Alert email or log message when nexus is triggered includes text matching 'ACTION REQUIRED: Economic nexus triggered in {state}'

checklist.name: 80% early warning
checklist.description: An early warning alert is sent/logged when gross sales reach 80% of the sales threshold, before nexus is fully triggered

checklist.name: Register at 80% guidance
checklist.description: README or code comments document that registration should happen at 80% of threshold, not after it's crossed, because nexus is retroactive from trigger date

checklist.name: Upsert pattern
checklist.description: Threshold updates use an upsert-style operation (create record if not exists, increment if exists) keyed on state_code and tracking_year

checklist.name: nexus_trigger_date recorded
checklist.description: When nexus is triggered, the trigger date/timestamp is recorded (not just a boolean flag)

checklist.name: Zero-nexus tracking
checklist.description: When an order arrives from a state with no current nexus, the order is still tracked toward the threshold (totals are incremented)

checklist.name: OR logic for thresholds
checklist.description: Nexus is triggered when EITHER the sales threshold OR the transaction count threshold is met (not requiring both to be met)
